response.justification: The candidate is a seasoned, versatile engineer with genuine high-performance systems experience, but the core requirement of this role is deep, current C++ expertise applied to industrial automation, and that is missing. His only professional C++ work was a roughly seven-month stint at National Instruments in 2014-2015; the past decade is dominated by TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang product and web work. There is no evidence of MQTT, message-broker architectures in C++, industrial protocols, or IEC 62443 / Secure Development Lifecycle practice, which are central to this position.
strengths: Real low-latency, concurrent systems experience (async I/O, fibers, caching) handling millions of requests per second at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic
Strong engineering-craft culture: TDD, CI/CD, authored test frameworks, and a track record of mentoring through workshops and courses
React and Node.js proficiency plus an event-driven, Kubernetes-deployable platform, matching several of the advantageous skills
concerns: C++ experience is minimal and stale: about seven months professionally, ending in 2015, against a role demanding hands-on C++/STL architecture
No exposure to industrial automation, IoT protocols (MQTT, OPC UA, Modbus), or IEC 62443 / SDL secure-engineering standards
Career trajectory centers on web and full-stack product work, a significant retooling distance from networked, multi-threaded industrial C++ platforms

key_factors.factor: Industrial cybersecurity and IoT protocol experience
